item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This teeters on the edge of not being possible to take seriously, and frankly probably should have fallen over into -J territory, but given the low threat this anomaly holds I can see low-level personnel having a smidge of fun with it. I had fun reading it too. +1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it originated from here, with no indication it was released under Creative Commons. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like it. Hello there, Bilious Slick!

Upvoted. It was solidly written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this because I like cordial conversations with anomalies, and contemplating the nature of the universe as a pacman frog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Universe frog w/ tumor

Is that a motherfucking Homestuck Reference?!?!

All joking aside, I really like this skip. I was expecting a rehash of SCP-1470 but I was pleasantly surprised by how unique this felt; while there are similarities, the personalities are different enough that this stands on its own. Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regardless of whether or not this changes your outtake on the interview, the last line was entirely supposed to be hokey. The very last thing on my mind when I wrote that final piece of dialogue was, 'dang this is gonna sound so cool and serious'. I was building up for a silly punch line, and there it was. Nothing quite says genesis ulcer like calling yourself the 'omniwart'.

Honestly, I've been unsure on how to touch this up further. I'd redrafted over and over, and significantly rewrote it before posting it here, and this is kinda just what ended up happening given me unsure of where to go from where I had it.

W h o o p s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty good but I feel like it falls down in the second interview. The researcher's replies are pretty stilted and he seems to be pretty clueless - the concepts being presented aren't so difficult that a researcher should have such trouble understanding them, and I found the last line a little hokey.
I like the concept though, and it's good up until that interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool ideas here. Really enjoy the frog's actions. However, you could make the description a bit clearer - I couldn't tell what 3594 actually is, only that 3594-A is a frog.

By the way, It this a Homestuck reference?

long link collapsed, for the sake of our mobile readers
hide

mspaintadventures.wikia.com%2Fwiki%2FGenesis_Frog&psig=AOvVaw3D3nTdJ0pPefkmF7pGzUKH&ust=1524526007463404

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly wasn't sure if I wanted to post it or not, but after a few more minor tweaks and a few brush ups, I said fuck it and aired on the side of posting it live.

item.title: I am the very model of a modern major Anura
item.content:encoded: An enjoyable read. The last line got a smile out of me. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hehe. The last line gets me every time.
+1

item.title: Author Notes
item.content:encoded:
Alright, this marks my second article that's going up on the mainlist. Honestly? I was really broken up about whether or not I actually wanted to post this or not, though alas here it is.
An idea birthed from shitposting.

God west my souwl.
